body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` (Azure Management SDK Generator) **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** commit 371a2adb3596d0a397ef7516aed96e5ae27501d1 (#58291) by @ArcturusZhang ## What Changed PR #58291 unified scope information into a single `ArmScopeInfo` record type (renamed from a prior type) and added a `Scope: ArmScopeInfo` parameter to `NonResourceMethod`. Extension non-resource operations now carry full scope info (kind, ID pattern, resource type) through the generation pipeline, enabling correct scope parameter injection in generated mockable extension methods. Key files changed: - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/ArmScopeInfo.cs` ŌĆö renamed and updated unified scope record - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/NonResourceMethod.cs` ŌĆö now carries `ArmScopeInfo Scope` - `MockableArmClientProvider.cs` and `MockableResourceProvider.cs` ŌĆö use scope info to emit the scope parameter ## Gaps Found - `docs/OperationContext.md` documents extension resources briefly (┬¦ "Extension Resources") but does not mention `ArmScopeInfo` at all, nor does it explain how scope is resolved for **non-resource** methods on extension clients - Neither `docs/OperationContext.md` nor `docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` documents the `NonResourceMethod` model or how its `Scope` field drives generated method signatures - There is no documentation of the `ArmScopeInfo` record itself: its three fields (`Kind`, `ScopeIdPattern`, `ScopeResourceType`), when `ScopeResourceType` is null, and how it differs from the per-resource `ResourceScope` enum
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` **Edit** ŌĆö extend the existing "Extension Resources" section and add a new "Non-Resource Methods" subsection after "Singleton Resources": ```markdown ### ArmScopeInfo `ArmScopeInfo` is a unified record that describes the ARM scope of any operation: | Field | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | `Kind` | `ResourceScope` | Scope kind: `Tenant`, `Subscription`, `ResourceGroup`, `ManagementGroup`, or `Extension` | | `ScopeIdPattern` | `RequestPathPattern` | The parsed request path that identifies the scope (e.g., `/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}`) | | `ScopeResourceType` | `string?` | The ARM resource type of the scope (e.g., `"Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines"`). **Null** when the scope is tenant-level (empty path) or when the path contains variable resource type segments | `ArmScopeInfo` replaces the previous per-resource scope representations with a single, consistent type shared by both resource and non-resource operations. ### Non-Resource Methods Some Azure operations are **not** associated with a specific ARM resource type. They are grouped under the `NonResourceMethod` model: ```csharp public record NonResourceMethod( ArmScopeInfo Scope, InputServiceMethod InputMethod, InputClient InputClient) ``` The `Scope` field carries the scope at which the operation is valid. For example, a subscription-scoped non-resource list operation will have `Scope.Kind == ResourceScope.Subscription`. #### How Scope Drives Method Signatures for Non-Resource Extension Methods When a non-resource method's scope kind is `Extension` (i.e., it can be applied to any ARM resource), the generator: 1. Reads `Scope.ScopeIdPattern` to determine whether a `scope` parameter (`ResourceIdentifier`) is needed. 2. If the pattern is variable (not fixed to a specific resource type), the generated method in `MockableArmClientProvider` receives an explicit `ResourceIdentifier scope` parameter. 3. If `Scope.ScopeResourceType` is non-null, the generator can infer the scope type, and the parameter may be omitted or defaulted. **Example ŌĆö generated method signature for an extension non-resource list:** ```csharp // Scope is Extension with variable ScopeIdPattern ŌåÆ explicit scope parameter public virtual AsyncPageable GetFoosAsync( ResourceIdentifier scope, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) ``` Compare to a resource-group-scoped operation where scope is contextual and the parameter is elided: ```csharp // Scope is ResourceGroup ŌåÆ scope derived from Id public virtual AsyncPageable GetFoosAsync( CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) ``` ``` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` **Edit** ŌĆö add an "ArmScopeInfo and NonResourceMethod" subsection to the "Data Structure Mapping" section: ```markdown ### ArmScopeInfo (unified scope type) `ArmScopeInfo` is produced by the emitter for both resource scopes and non-resource method scopes. It is serialised into `tspCodeModel.json` and deserialised by the C# generator: ```typescript interface ArmScopeInfo { kind: ResourceScope; // e.g. "Extension", "ResourceGroup", "Subscription" scopeIdPattern: string; // parsed request path for the scope scopeResourceType?: string; // ARM resource type, null for tenant or variable types } ``` ### NonResourceMethod (scope-aware) Non-resource methods are serialised with a `scope` field that contains the `ArmScopeInfo` for the operation: ```typescript interface NonResourceMethod { methodId: string; scope: ArmScopeInfo; } ``` The scope field is **never null** ŌĆö every non-resource method must have a resolvable scope before it is included in the schema. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No code snippets are needed for these documentation-only changes. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The files being updated are generator-internal developer docs under `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/`, not a customer-facing SDK README. No standard README section check applies. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/` ŌĆö ensures no C# compilation regressions 2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/test/Azure.Generator.Mgmt.Tests.csproj` ŌĆö runs unit tests (168 tests expected) 3. No snippet or API export scripts are needed for documentation-only changes in `docs/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Mgmt Generator] Docs: Document ArmScopeInfo and scope handling for extension non-resource methods
